title: The Halloween Party
wikitext:
{{Fix|Light mode incompatibility; specifically, low contrast between text and background, which prevents comfortable reading.|date=2, July, 2026}} __NOTOC__ {{DISPLAYTITLE:The Halloween Party}} {{Halloween Theme}} {{Background|file=Burning house.jpeg}}
'''"Hell need not be a a lake of fire. It's a concept, a state of mind. With the diverse lives each of us lead, hell can take [[Level List|many forms]]. Hell will test us, break us, physically and emotionally, past anything we've seen in [[The Frontrooms|the world we call our own]]. Perhaps, in our modern, and increasingly isolated world, all Hell need be for some of us is [[Level 0|an empty, yellow office]].'''
'''What Hell is ''not'', is a playground for idiots. It is not another mere sandbox, waiting to be butchered by the farmer, it is not another battleground for us to conquer and burn to the ground. We had those chances with our old home, and just like the, greedy, cruel, disgusting little vermin you are, you took it. And that is why you are here. Here, there will be no more of your greed. There will be no more of your evil. And, as retribution for your crimes against all that is good, there will be no more of your happiness.'''
'''And that is where we come in. We see your attempts to colonise and comfort yourselves in this prison, and look upon you with distain. You look around, see that this world hates you, see how hostile it is to you, see how every single surface wants you dead! And what do you do? You try to fight it. Do you call this justice? You need to learn that you have failed your lives. And since you have no intent to follow the order that this place demands, we have taken it upon ourselves to get the job done for you."


-[[Clyde Nelson]], extract from his speech at the Crimson Carnival, 2018.
[[File:Costume11.svg]]
'''WELCOME TO HELL'''
'''YOU WON'T ENJOY YOUR STAY'''
{{Group Class|hostility=10|influence=4|organization=4|group type=Terrorist}} {{Group Infobox|capital=The Ghost Town|title1=The Halloween Party|religion=Satanism|ideology=Theocracy/Kraterocracy|habitat=Urban levels|languages=|foundation=2018|image1=costume11.svg|caption-image1=The official logo of The Halloween Party}} ==Description== '''The Halloween Party''' (sometimes referred to as '''The Hallowe'en Party''', '''THP''', or simply '''The Party''') is a terrorist and arsonist organisation within the backrooms, known for its operations throughout many inhabited levels which most commonly end in either untimely deaths, or perhaps worse fates, for their unfortunate victims. They have many tactics to carry out optimal destruction, such as raids, invasions, even down to mere vandalism, though the primary danger posed to civilians is for their twisted version of trick-or-treating. This involves patrols of around 25 of their members going around the backrooms, searching for human residences or lone wanderers and demanding supplies from them. These supplies can occasionally be as simple as sweets or chocolate, but more commonly they demand weapons such as knives and firearms, or even cannons. Should the wanderer(s) refuse, the Party will punish the wanderer, usually by burning their home down and either killing them or kidnapping them to be tortured back at one of their outposts. In rare instances, unlucky wanderers can be asked to give The Halloween Party entirely unrealistic items such as nuclear warheads or a live tyrannosaurus rex, essentially ensuring their demise. Most members of '''The Halloween Party''' are [[Phenomenon 35-A|Jovials]] and tamed [[Jack-O-Lanterns]], though they have occasionally been known to employ [[Entity 3|smilers]], [[Glowfolk]], and even some [[Dossier on the Common Raider|human gangs]]. The [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] has previously attempted to infiltrate their ranks using spies posing as an arsonist gang, though even without being caught they ended up being eaten by cannibalistic Jovials. It is for this reason that even if one supports their ideals, it is not recommended to join the group. Very few wish to voluntarily join '''The Halloween Party''' without indoctrination, which takes different forms depending on species. [[Phenomenon 35-A|Jovials]] do not require much, since much of their control is induced by [[Phenomenon 35-A#Telepathic Suggestion|Telepathic Suggestion]], but to make absolutely certain that they never falter, they go through a seven month conditioning course, dubbed by '''The Party''' as "The Blasphemous Briefing". This course ranges in length, being only two months for [[Jack-O-Lanterns]] and up to two years for [[Entity 3|Smilers]] and [[Glowfolk]], and anything in between for any other entity members. Humans are the only exception as most bypass the course altogether, instead being recruited and indoctrinated by a sub-group:
[[File:churchforgottenhalls.png|right|frameless]] ===The Church of the Forgotten Halls=== The Church of the Forgotten Halls is a self proclaimed christian charity organisation dedicated to helping those who have recently entered the backrooms and groups that have recently suffered damage from entities and conflict with hostile groups. In fact, the organisation is nothing more than a puppet group used to indoctrinate unsuspecting recruits into '''The Party'''. The group's marketed beliefs stem from their own branch of Christianity which views the backrooms as a fourth plane in addition to Heaven, Hell, and Earth, where the morally neutral are sent to receive further judgement by God after their deaths. This religion is purely a work of fiction invented by its high ranking members to make it seem more like a legitimate aid organisation, and the only ones who actually believe it are low ranking members, who are unknowingly acting as pawns in a pyramid scheme to recruit more future terrorists. Once indoctrination reaches a certain stage and the recruit has full trust in the COTFH, they are then admitted to the second stage and sent to a re-education camp to become loyal members of '''The Halloween Party'''. By this point, most groups have evicted The Church of the Forgotten Halls from their territory, any that have not are urged to take immediate action, and contact the [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] if they lack the manpower to forcibly evict them. Recently, due to being exposed for their crimes, the puppet group has been creating puppet groups of its own to avoid detection. As a general rule, it is advised to take extreme caution around any groups that offer aid, and if one such groups tries to convert you into their religion, even if it is an established religion in [[The Frontrooms]], leave immediately. Do not announce your departure, instead quietly leave as soon as possible. '''Do not trust the church.'''
==Hierarchy== ===The lords of Hell=== The aptly named Lords of Hell are the elite council of '''The Party''', who are rarely seen even by the group's highest officials. Almost all sightings of them by non members are during the organisation's 'Crimson Carnival' celebration, more on which can be found in this document. It is thought that this group is the sole force maintaining order throughout their forces, making them prime targets for larger groups such as the [[Backroom Colonists]], as killing them would likely destabilise the entire group. Interestingly, the council is led not by a [[Sanguine Festivus]] victim, nor a [[Jack-O-Lanterns|Jack-o'-lantern]], but a [[Phenomenon 35|wretch]] named [[Clyde Nelson]] who by unknown means retains his ability to coherently think and communicate, though he does suffer from frequent panic attacks and bursts of insanity. Clyde has previously stated his distain for large groups and the protection they provide, starting that "all the rats sent to sent to this bloody hellscape deserve to burn in it!" ===Reapers=== Reapers are the enforcers of the Lords of Hell, and are responsible for keeping other members in check, distinguished by the skythes they carry on their backs, and are trained to use them as lethal weapons. Typically reserved only for the strongest, smartest, and most loyal members, promotion to a Reaper grants one command of any members they desire. Seeing as how enforcement within the group is so loose, the command style and strictness of individual Reapers varies wildly, and when working together they frequently argue and fight for control. This is one of the main reasons to assume the group would cease to function should the Lords of Hell die, as the Halloween Party would probably split into many smaller criminal gangs fighting for power. ===Patrols=== {{Simple Image Block|image=The Halloween party.jpeg|caption=An eyewitness sketch of a patrol in level 9.}} Patrols make up the bulk of the organisation, comprised each of around 25 members each. Reapers rarely join patrols, instead appointing patrol commanders to act in their name, further creating division within the group. Patrols are tasked with raiding resources from civilians and large groups to maintain their supply lines and spread disarray and terror throughout their ranks. While procedure in patrols may vary, they usually allow their victims one minute to gather resources to give to them. If possible, use this time to escape, as not only does giving them resources allow them to take more lives, but more eccentric [[Sanguine Festivus]] victims are known not to keep their word and attack no matter what they are given. Patrols are also tasked with monitoring [[Level 9]] for any newly placed [[Jack-O-Lanterns]], which are taken back to their main base to be tamed and raised to be loyal to the Halloween Party. ===Grunts=== Grunts are the lowest ranked members of the group, comprised mostly of new recruits that just completed conditioning. Grunts spend most of their time in major outposts, though they are called out when the Halloween Party launches an invasion, when far more soldiers are needed than are in a patrol. In order for a grunt to join a patrol and rise up the ranks, they must impress a Reaper or a patrol commander enough to be promoted. This is typically done during invasions by demonstrating strength and bravery. This makes invasions all the more dangerous, as the grunts are unlikely to show restraint even if it involves risking their lives in the process. ==Culture== Members of The Halloween Party are, as stated earlier, fanatically loyal to their superiors, many members have been seen risking their lives for their commanders even in cases where they display a clear disliking towards them. It is theorised that this is due to the conditioning they go through before being assigned to their roles. Despite the harsh conditioning and extreme loyalty, enforcement of rules within the group is extremely lenient. Members frequently get away with killing or injuring their targets unprovoked and behaving recklessly with their weapons.they won't do this if given a direct order not to ===Attire=== While they don't enforce a dress code, many humanoid members of the group choose to wear halloween costumes. This is particularly popular among humans and less disfigured [[Phenomenon 35-A|jovials]] as an alternate way to strike fear into their victims. This has since proven ineffective, but it remains popular among many members as a means of showing their allegiance to the group. Patrol commanders usually wear highwayman uniforms as a way of displaying their authority. Capes are extremely common among members of the group, most [[Jack-O-Lanterns]] use it as their only clothing, opting instead to keep their skeletal bodies exposed to their victims. ===Satanism=== Almost all members of '''The Halloween Party''' follow some form of satanism, though unlike what the Lords of Hell claim, the exact beliefs of the population vary wildly even if staying broadly inside of the satanism label. ====Halloween Party Satanism==== The group's official religion, according to the lords of Hell. Supposedly shared across the group, although in practice this is clearly untrue. Most members of the lords of Hell were orthodox christians, who at some point in their exploration of the backrooms came to the conclusion that they were in Hell and that they along with anyone else trapped here deserves to suffer. Due to this, they have a distain for large groups that provide stability and safety in the backrooms, such as [[M.E.G.]] and [[The Database]], both of which are prime targets of the organisation. They see themselves as the enforcers of Hell, claiming it is their rightful duty to bring pain and suffering to all humans in the backrooms. '''Halloween Party''' satanism is focused on the worship of two deities: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/God_the_Father God], of the Christian bible, and [https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Satan&ved=2ahUKEwiCr9CHlZSUAxVTUkEAHZTvMpEQFnoECCIQAQ&usg=AOvVaw04xwBWicMiGsYzUB8UqJWc Satan] of the abrahamic faiths, who the Lords of Hell claim is a worthy idol, both the king and the primary prisoner of Hell, and worthy of both titles. While the Lords of Hell acknowledge his misdeeds, they praise him for embracing his immorality and eternal punishment, for which '''The Party''' deem him, "the divine punisher". In fact, both "God" and "Satan" are believed to be manipulators who indoctrinated Nelson. Possible identities for Satan include [[The Halloween Demon]], [[Entity 18|The Beast of Level 5]], or perhaps [[Entity 333|The Serpent]]. Possible identities for "God" are fewer, and the theories that do exist vary wildly and lack much substantial evidence. ====Gruntfolk Satanism==== In reality, however much the Lords of Hell claim otherwise, '''The Halloween Party''''s general population are much more apathetic towards religion. Their training focuses little on religion, and more so on the general ethos of the members' righteousness above all other beings as enforcers of Hell, and making them have no hesitation to do as they please to non-members. Whether or not they genuinely believe in their officially designated religion is mostly irrelevant to their leaders. This majority follow Gruntfolk Satanism, a broad term of '''The Halloween Party''''s [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Satanism#Nontheistic_Satanism nontheistic satanists], who see Satan as a brand or a mascot rather than as a genuine deity. While there are some slight variations around the theme, most see the meaning of life as freedom from rules, which includes morals, which they view as an abstract and nonsensical cog in the bureaucracy that is society. To them, Satan represents individual pride, freedom to do evil, and freedom from societies' legal and moral obligations. Note that this belief is not inherently sadistic, rather it serves as a justification and embracement for and of said sadism. They see themselves as being in a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Social_contract social contract] with their commanders in which they freely choose to sacrifice some agency in return for the vast resources, strategy, and somewhat stable likeminded community '''The Halloween Party''' maintains through its existence. Gruntfolk Satanism is by far the most popular belief amongst the general population, believed by the vast majority of grunts (hence the name), patrol members, and patrol commanders, as well as most Reapers. One of the Lords of Hell, a Jack-O-Lantern named [[Clyde Nelson#Mephistopheles|Mephistopheles]] is also rumoured to be a Gruntfolk Satanist. ===The Crimson Carnival=== The Halloween Party holds many festivals throughout the year, the most prominent being their role in the [[Jack-O-Lanterns]]' frightening festival. Since the group's establishment in 2018, the Halloween Party has always offered to host the opening of the festival with an event they call 'The Crimson Carnival' on the 31st of October, coinciding with the widespread Halloween festival in the frontrooms. The celebrations are held at every Halloween Party base, with the carnival itself being held at the group's capital, the Ghost Town (see Bases, colonies and outposts). During this time, many high profile figures in the backrooms come to join the celebrations such as [[Caius Taggert|Sir Caius Orpheus Taggert]] and [[Jack Umbra]], who are treated as guests of honour. Activities include parading the hostages they have taken throughout the year around the base, where members are encouraged to barter highly valued [[Object List|objects]] in return for seeing the hostages brutally tortured, and skull painting (decorating genuine human skulls to resemble pumpkins, similar to the head of [[The Halloween Demon]]). This event is one of the rare times the lords of Hell reveal themselves, with [[Clyde Nelson]] giving an annual speech, usually praising the group's members for supposedly upholding the natural order of the backrooms and spreading the misery and pain that humans in the backrooms deserve to feel. ==Dead Man's Steeds== A dead man's steed is a horse-like creature ridden by members of '''The Halloween Party''', described as black skeletons of horses, with only minor areas of flesh located around the joints of their bones. Sometimes they are used by entire patrols, or sometimes just by the patrol commander while the rest of the patrol walk. They are also used in ceremonial duties such as military parades, as well as pulling carriages of the Lords of Hell or well-known Reapers. No corpse of a dead man's steed has ever been recovered, leaving it entirely unknown what they are. It is thought that '''The Halloween Party''''s supply of them is limited, however, as they are not used on a regular basis and patrols that do use them for all their members tend to be significantly smaller. The most common theories on what they are are: *A creation of [[Clyde Nelson]]. This is generally considered the least likely due to the above evidence *A donation from "God" or "Satan" *Biological Beings. As strange as it sounds, the bones seen on the outside may be an exoskeleton for the creature within. ==Relationships== ===Allies/Neutral=== Due to the inherently hostile nature of the group, almost all groups hold negative relations with them. They cannot be said to hold true alliances, but there are a few groups that The Party makes exceptions to their usual policies on human life for, and even occasionally collaborates with. ===[[Atrego|Ecotopia of Atrego]]=== Atrego is one of the few groups The Halloween Party respects, as both groups believe anarchy is the best form of governance in the backrooms. Both groups have a shared hatred of larger organisations and have participated in joint raids and invasions against them several times. Of course, members of the Halloween Party can and have killed and tortured citizens of Atrego just as they would do to any human, but this is deemed as acceptable within Atrego as they believe the backrooms should be a place where it is everyone for themselves, and that killing and bloodshed is a necessity to The Halloween Party's members.This sentiment is not shared throughout the group. Occasionally, high profile figures within Atrego will speak out against The Halloween Party's abhorrent actions. These members are usually assassinated by The Halloween Party. Citizens of Atrego have criticised The Halloween Party for their rigid command structure and permanent bases, claiming that they are not true anarchists. However due to the group's lack of allies they are willing to put aside their differences and cooperate when organising raids on their enemies. ===[[Caius Taggert|The Partygoer Jamboree]]=== Initially, Taggert and [[Sanguine Festivus|his army]] saw The Halloween Party as enemies attempting to steal their soldiers, as they both make attempts to attract large numbers of [[Phenomenon 35-A|Jovials]] to their sides, and thus they proved to be nothing but a strain on each others' recruitment operations. However, over time, several factors have bought them closer together. Of course, there is a mutual love of chaos, but there is also a shared hatred of the [[Party Crashers]]. Other than the Jamboree, '''The Halloween Party''' has the highest number of [[Phenomenon 35-A|Jovials]] in a single group, and thus are a prime target for Party Crasher members. This has prompted several cases of the two groups working together in order to combat the threat the Crashers pose to them. Another thing holding the two groups together is The Halloween Party's export of [[Sanguine Festivus]] victims to the Jamboree. Due to the high Jovial population, infection of prisoners is extremely common, and in many cases encouraged. Not only does this fulfil The Halloween Party's main goal as the victims are kept without food in constant hunger and agony, but the products of said torture practice also serve a secondary purpose: once the victims arrive at their most distraught mental state, they will either develop [[Phenomenon 35-A|Jovial Syndrome]] in rare cases, in which case they are taken for conditioning to keep them loyal to '''The Halloween Party''', or more commonly, they will remain as they are. While the Lords of Hell can telepathically influence and condition [[Phenomenon 35-A|Jovials]], [[Sanguine Festivus|regular victims]] are of no further use to them. This is not the case for [[Caius Taggert]], who can mind control them and force them to join The Partygoer Jamboree. Because of this, the two groups are in a deal where '''The Halloween Party''' will export bulk supplies of [[Sanguine Festivus]] victims to The Jamboree in return for large sums of money, food, and weapons. Seeing as no other group produces this many infected victims as a byproduct from their operationsExcept, perhaps, The Uninvited in [[Level 161]], who would be completely unwilling for relations with either group., '''The Halloween Party''' essentially has a monopoly on a trade for a very demanding client. ===[[CJNF|Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun]]=== Relations between '''The Halloween Party''' and The CJNF have so far displayed neutral relations, though the two have remained very open to future collaboration. Like with The Partygoer Jamboree, the CJNF is also a major customer of '''The Halloween Party''''s [[Sanguine Festivus]] victim market, as some of their [[New Fun|Celebrants]] are created from them. There is significant tension from the fact that '''The Halloween Party''' participates in diplomacy and trading with both the CJNF and The Partygoer Jamboree, as the two groups are fierce rivals and are currently in a Cold War, and by aligning himself with either of them, they risk receiving trade sanctions from the other. Insider reports claim that should they have to choose, The Lords of Hell are extremely divided on which faction they would back, though, notably, they do want one of them to prevail as should they fall, nothing would stop '''The Halloween Party''' from becoming The [[Party Crashers]]' primary target. The following is an extract from [[Clyde Nelson]]'s stolen diary, this section written in early 2024. Note the more informal tone compared to his usual demeanour:
File - NelsonDiary22.txt ''Why? Why can't those idiots just stop their bickering? If they'd just stay in their lane, just kept doing their bit to make Hell as Hellish as possible, then everything would be fine! But no! that [[The Party Creator|purple Partygoer]] just has to have daddy issues! Now I hear they want a war on each other any year now, yeah, I'm sure that's gonna do wonders for all of us! And, of course, they'll both be fighting for us as their ally and force us to cut one of them off! ''And of course, when one of them prevails, the other will inevitably be rendered weak and be taken out themselves! And then, who's gonna be left next on the [[Party Crashers|Crashers']] chopping block? Oh, yeah! Us! Just the news we needed!''
===[[level 161|The Eternal Flame]]=== Perhaps the only group to surpass even '''The Halloween Party''''s hatred of human civilisation, The Eternal Flame is probably their closest ally. They very frequently share intel and jointly participate in raids, and have given aid to their forces in The War of Level 248. Of course, like with all their other "allies", they are still driven apart by ideological differences, mainly that while The Eternal Flame prefers to kill as many humans as quickly and efficiently as possible, '''The Halloween Party''' would rather draw out the process. This is because their end goal is not, as is commonly suggested, the death of humans, but the suffering of them, and thus swiftly and painlessly killing their targets is not satisfactory to them unless absolutely necessary. This often leads to arguments between the logically minded Glowfolk and the sadistic minds of '''The Halloween Party'''. Another form of tension stems from '''The Halloween Party''''s willingness to employ human forces. While [[Phenomenon 35-A|jovials]] and [[Phenomenon 35|wretches]] are just about acceptable, The Eternal Flame outright refuses any assistance to operations involving standard humans. ===Enemies=== It is infeasible to list every enemy of The Halloween Party. In total, it would require listing almost every major inter-level faction and an extremely high number of single-level groups. Hence, groups listed here are the ones that had the most hostility to the group or groups that The Halloween Party has had a particular focus on. ===[[Backrooms Investigation Foundation]]=== Seeing as the primary focus of the B.I.F. is stopping criminal gangs and lawbreakers, it and The Halloween Party have become fierce enemies of each other, [[Clyde Nelson]] currently being the B.I.F.'s public enemy #1. Members of both groups are ordered to kill members of the other on sight, with occasional exceptions for interrogation purposes. The lords of Hell believe the B.I.F. are traitors to the order of God by attempting to bring peace to those who aren't worthy of it. ===[[Recovered Data File|Marauders]]=== Despite their similar goals of destroying human civilisations, The Halloween Party leadership strongly dislike the Marauders due to an incident in which a former Lord of Hell believed them to be demons sent by the Devil, and it was the duty of the Party to see them as their masters. These claims were shot down by [[Clyde Nelson]], but nevertheless a large splinter faction dedicated to the Marauders developed, leading the group into a civil war between 2021 and 2022, ending with the main faction ultimately regaining their control over the group. The Marauders themselves have no official relations with the Halloween Party. ===[[Royal Exploration Unit]]=== {{Simple Image Block|image=French stuff.webp|caption=A Halloween Party arson attack in Livingston, the regional capital of the Lumber District of [[Level 248]].}} The R.E.U. and '''The Halloween Party''' were recently in warfare against each other over control of the R.E.U.'s home level, [[Level 248]]. This occurred after a rise in their terrorist activity around the level, prompting military intervention and escalation from both sides, as well as involvement of [[Atrego]], resulting in full invasion attempts by Atrego and '''The Halloween Party'''. Although the Waris now over, the resentment against '''The Party''' remains, and the R.E.U. remain firmly committed to removing their presence from both their homeland and other levels they bring havoc to. ===[[Party Crashers]]=== '''The Halloween Party''' holds the third largest [[Phenomenon 35-A|Jovial]] population in the backrooms, behind only the CJNF and The Partygoer Jamboree. It is thus their third most prioritised target behind those groups, leading to frequent clashes between the groups. The Party Crashers are generally seen as the second best group to provide assistance to anyone under threat from '''The Halloween Party'''. It is strongly advised to either contact them or the B.I.F. in the event of an attack. ===Anti Entity Group=== As a large group comprised mostly of hostile entities, the A.E.G. is naturally highly opposed to them. They frequently collaborate with the [[Party Crashers]] and [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] to combat their presence. '''The Halloween Party''''s existence has somewhat benefitted the A.E.G. in regards to publicity and the increase in organised entity violence has brought many to their side. In an interview with [[User:Chrisonmagic/rnl.org|Royal Newsletter]], an A.E.G. spokesperson said the following quote, which by now has become iconic amongst many: {{Quote|Now, do you all finally see? This is why we have to end the humanisation of entities now! They are not like us on a fundamental level. The entities finally manage to band together to form some semblance of civilisation, and what do they do with it? Immediately begin mass terorist operations against humankind.Many of the A.E.G.'s sceptics are keen to point out the irony, as the A.E.G are a group of humans, and their primary goal is extinction of entities. This is not a one-off. Look how the U.G.A. and the eternal flame turned out on [[Level 161|161]]. Entities cannot do good deeds. They cannot help us. And so we have the right to return the favour. The time for mercy against these freaks of nature is at an end. We hope that now, with the force of a militarised terror group teeming with entities on full display, you can see that too, and join our cause to resist their brutality. |Richard Montague, spokesperson for the A.E.G.}} ==Bases, colonies, and outposts== ===The Ghost Town=== The main base and de facto capital of The Halloween Party, The Ghost Town's name is somewhat ironic as it is in fact (according to survivors and prisoners) a very lively place. The central square of the city takes on the aesthetic of a medieval European settlement, but other areas of it take on other aesthetics associated with Halloween such as American suburbs and a haunted funfair, which is especially popular around the Crimson Carnival. Aside from these, The Ghost Town also has more practical buildings such as science laboratories, rudimentary healthcare facilities, and a large mental asylum complex where all new recruits are taken for "cultural assimilation". As described by the B.I.F., the city is "armed to the teeth", with '''The Halloween Party''''s military might on full display. Large areas of the city are reserved for barracks and training facilities as well as storage for their tanks and other military vehicles. They sometimes hold military parades in the centre of the city to boost internal morale and as a publicity stunt to other groups (the parades are uploaded to [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Virus Video]] and quickly circulate amongst users of the [[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]]. The Ghost Town is believed to house about 5000 members, with a further 1500 members in patrols that are stationed there. It is located on an unknown level, though it is widely believed to be in [[Level 9]] or one of its sublevels. The city is centred around a large castle, officially named "The Sinner Sanctum"A pun on "sinner" and "inner sanctum", that serves as the residence of the Lords of Hell. This building features a balcony overlooking a central square, where [[Clyde Nelson]] makes his annual speeches. The castle is decorated with Halloween decorations all year round, and displays a sculpture of a jack-o'-lantern face on its front. Other than these, the castle takes on an old fashioned medieval appearance. Despite this astetic, the castle is rigged with modern technology including CCTV cameras and traps hidden in the walls. Many areas also require a fingerprint scan to enter. It is unknown what is behind those doors. The base is governed by the Lords of Hell and by approximately 70 Reapers. This is unusual as most bases only have 1 to 3 Reapers in command. ===The Royal Satanic Legion=== The Royal Satanic Legion is a small outpost hidden somewhere in [[Level 248]] consisting of around 70 members, most in patrols. Recently, the base saw a surge of activity as '''The Halloween Party''' launched a full scale war against the R.E.U. homeland, in which they successfully occupied much of the island's New Scottish districts for a substantial time. Now, however, what remains of them are rarely seen and spend most of their time in hiding from the [[Royal Exploration Unit]]. Other than the war, they are mainly known for their consistent arson attacks on the R.E.U.'s Tall Tom building every 5th of November, coinciding with the British celebration of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guy_Fawkes_Night Bonfire Night]. ===Cult of The Free=== The "Cult of The Free" is a medium sized colony in [[Level 594]], containing approximately 200 members and based on the level's version of the American capital, Washington D.C., though they often move around to other areas. They specialise in finding planes, missiles and other war machines from Alternate America's abandoned military bases, which make up the bulk of the Halloween Party's military strength. It is worthy to note that members of this colony are all [[Jack-O-Lanterns]] as [[Phenomenon 35-A|jovials]] succumb to the level's body altering hazards as they are still technically human. ===[[Level 9/Bases and Outposts#Devil's Dawn|Devil's Dawn]]=== Devil's Dawn is a large colony located in [[Level 9]] with a population of approximately 1000, about half of which are in patrols. The base functions as a city for The Halloween Party and their secondary main base of operations. The base mainly exists to recruit new members as it is one of the few places where it is possible to find one day old (and thus tameable) [[Jack-O-Lanterns]]. This base is identifiable as a group of 6 mansionsIt is theorised that these are dedicated to the Lords of Hell as back when the base was constructed they had six members as opposed to the five they have now.in level 9 covered in Halloween decorations and surrounded by a wall topped with barbed wire, though it also features a large underground bunker that houses many of the lower ranking members. The base is interesting to many rival groups as it contains a portal that directly links to The Ghost Town, this being the only way enemy spies and escapees have gone to and from it. Unless absolutely necessary, it is not recommended to look for this portal, as it is usually heavily guarded on both sides. But, if one must find it, it is located in the largest of the six mansions in the dining room. Exact descriptions of the portal are vague, but most agree it is hard to miss. Once one goes through it, the teleportation process takes 5 minutes, during which time wanderers will begin traveling through a space similar in appearance to [[The Broken]], though it isn't dangerous to travel through. That being said, it can trigger epileptic seasures and has been described as nauseating. Upon arrival, one will find themselves in The Ghost Town's castle in a similar dining room. More information on Devil's Dawn can be found [[Level 9/Bases and Outposts|here]]. ==Military== The Halloween Party's military is, ''amorphous'' to say the least. While being majorly led by '''THP''' officials, it is comprised mostly of scattered militia groups armed with weapons from [[Level 594]], such as the [https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M16_rifle&ved=2ahUKEwiThs2ThuKUAxXIVEEAHZirMKMQFnoECB8QAQ&usg=AOvVaw2He3vzDbK96RHskz6XIZr3 M16A2] and [https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M14_rifle&ved=2ahUKEwiI8O6phuKUAxULV0EAHfOwMAQQFnoECCwQAQ&usg=AOvVaw0cP59mTuV01P2ssbJcJVfe M14], while employing guerilla tactics and unconventional warfare. Members are often somewhat untrained, focusing on fear rather than skill. However, their disorganization is not to be confused with incompetence, and their armies are a force to be reckoned with. As stated before, '''Halloween Party''' members primarily form patrols, in which they raid small encampments and/or residences in populated areas. In combat operations, similar groups are formed, consisting of a few small fireteams led by one squad leader, likewise appointed by a Reaper. Alternatively, these militant squadrons are known as Hunter Squads. These Hunter Squads often work by splitting into their fireteams, surrounding and ambushing enemies with lethal efficiency. '''The Halloween Party''' has very little armored support, mainly using modified civilian vehicles and few [https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Armoured_personnel_carrier&ved=2ahUKEwjCiKHNhuKUAxUqVUEAHU2-ELIQFnoECCMQAQ&usg=AOvVaw0dPYFiuJYk5ujfoe7MH5f_ APC's] and [https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Infantry_fighting_vehicle&ved=2ahUKEwiU5MXshuKUAxWwdUEAHdH3BzYQFnoECBsQAQ&usg=AOvVaw3rtmUM95vn60sDv4nkv-w4 IFV's]. Despite apparently having access to stronger vehicles like [https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_battle_tank&ved=2ahUKEwj27e2Kh-KUAxX8Q0EAHUKXGvoQFnoECCgQAQ&usg=AOvVaw3smOrF2T9GqJzHcljGB98J. MBT's] and fixed and rotary wing aircraft, reports claim that '''THP''' does not use them due to lack of training and an inability to be effective with '''The Halloween Party''''s preferred way of fighting battles, in addition to poor maintenance causing many failures in operation. Regardless, the THP does not allow other groups acquire the unused vehicles, likely due to strategic advantage. '''The Halloween Party''' does, however, favor anti-tank/air weapon platforms such as the [https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AT4&ved=2ahUKEwiA1Pi1h-KUAxUtQUEAHU5HOaUQFnoECA0QAQ&usg=AOvVaw0ZG3p3NnRkq1icpkgemuN2 AT-4] and [https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FIM-92_Stinger&ved=2ahUKEwidiurLh-KUAxUNUkEAHe6XARYQFnoECCQQAQ&usg=AOvVaw3knqMc-N_IzCUCpmntEg6J Stinger platform]. Despite their skill in combat and gunfighting, many, if not most '''Halloween Party''' militants wear little to no camouflage or body armor. Most members opt to wear a Halloween costume in conforming with '''The Party''', and most (especially Jack-O-Lanterns) find helmets cumbersome and/or uncomfortable. As such, most members wear some sort of chest rig to carry magazines and supplies, with few wearing light Kevlar body armor underneath. The lack of camouflage clothing and gear makes them easy to spot in the distance, meaning it is advised to be on the lookout for any '''Halloween Party''' Hunter Squads. Due to the lack of armored support or air superiority, '''The Halloween Party''''s only considered military branch is the ground branch, simply known as the Halloween Insurgency. ==History== ===Pre 2018=== Although not yet established, various raiding groups compromised of [[Phenomenon 35-A|jovials]] formed during [[Phenomenon 12|the flicker]], using the confusion to weaken and attack groups which would under regular circumstances be able to defeat them with ease. After the flicker, many of these smaller disorganised factions would join '''The Halloween Party'''. During this time, it is also believed [[Clyde Nelson]] was raising his first generation of [[Jack-O-Lanterns|Jack-o'-Lantern]] loyalists and forming the Lords of Hell. ===2018-2019=== After establishment, '''The Halloween Party''' was initially mistaken by the [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation]] as just another criminal gang in the lawless lands of the backrooms. However, as more and more bases part of the group came forward and the B.I.F. verified footage of the group's large capital, a wave of mass panic hit the backrooms as various groups rushed to get information on this newly formed and very dangerous terrorist group. As a byproduct of the group's rising fame, the celebrating of Halloween plummeted in popularity throughout the backrooms, with various abrahamic fundamentalists pushing the view that Halloween is a satanic celebration, and gaining a rapid rise of support from civilians. This in turn caused a wave of hatred towards many who still celebrated Halloween, even children suffered from hate and violence from people who saw celebrating Halloween as siding with '''The Halloween Party'''. ===2020-2021=== Although the attacks from The Halloween Party persisted throughout this time, they were forced to spend more time fighting the previously mentioned civil war against a faction aligned with [[Recovered Data File|The Marauders]], known as The Guehenna Coalition, costing the group many soldiers and resources. This combined with groups gathering more information on the Party and predicting their attacks caused the group to become much less feared than they used to be. The popularity of Halloween slowly recovered during this time, but it was still considered somewhat controversial to celebrate it. It was the established of the Cult of The Free in late 2021 that really turned the tide of the war to '''The Party''''s favour. With the new supply of military vehicles and weapons, it took only a month to completely wipe out the rebellious splinter faction. The leader of it, a former Lord of Hell named [[Joseph Butcher]], was tortured to death in front of a large audience in the Crimson Carnival of 2022. ===2022-present=== With the splinter faction defeated and a stolen military at their disposal, '''The Halloween Party''' swiftly recovered to its former strength, sparking a new wave of hysteria and panic around the group. Many groups were caught off guard by '''The Halloween Party''''s rapid recovery, the B.I.F. included. They suffered many casualties as The Halloween Party wasted no time launching invasions of their major bases. One thing that groups did get right this time was saving the public perception of Halloween. To avoid a repeat of 2018, a campaign started by the [[M.E.G.]] was launched, aiming to distance the holiday from '''The Halloween Party'''. It was very successful, and the popularity of Halloween has increased, though not to the extent is previously had. To this day celebrating Halloween in the backrooms is still seen by some as a display of loyalty to '''The Halloween Party'''. In early 2026, '''The Halloween Party''' began its second war, this one against the [[Royal Exploration Unit]] with assistance from [[Atrego]], though in the middle of that war Atrego ended up betraying '''The Party'''. The conflict ended in an R.E.U. victory, but not without heavy casualties from all sides. The losses from this war, however, were nowhere near as great as those from the civil war. They have also recently seen a boost in population after the dissolution of [[Level 9/Bases and Outposts|The Maneaters]], resulting in '''The Halloween Party''' receiving an influx of refugees. The ideologies and practices of '''The Halloween Party''' and The Maneaters were very similar, and prior to their end they were very close allies. Although the group's end did cost '''The Party''' a valuable ally, it saw their population significantly rise as many of the group's surviving members sought membership of '''The Halloween Party''' to continue their way of life amongst others who live it too. To this day, '''The Halloween Party''' continues to strike fear and chaos across the backrooms. Their military strength, member count and number of raids on civilians are higher than ever, and the B.I.F. continues to hunt them down in hopes of one day defeating them. ==Survival Guide== If you ever encounter a patrol at your residence or are noticed by a member of the group, consult the following instructions. If you live in a level where they are known to have a base in, you may wish to print out these instructions and show others who live with you. These may be instrumental to your survival. ===Do=== *Stay quiet. You may be able to convince them your residence is uninhabited. *Escape via another exit (if possible) *Open the door, but '''only if they know you are there.''' They might agree to leave you time to gather supplies for them. Use this time to find an escape. If necessary, break open a window. *Contact or find a large group. They will be able to protect you. ===Don't=== *Try to hide. If they don't find you soon, they will probably destroy all potential hiding places until they find you. They could also set fire to your property with you trapped inside it. *Attempt to negotiate. They are regularly known to lie or to kill on sight. *Fight back except as a last resort. There are many heavily armed members with experience with weapons. You will almost certainly lose. *Meet their demands. Robbing wanderers is a vital part of their supply chain and giving them supplies will only endanger more innocent lives. Not to mention, they may betray you anyway. {{Collapse|final=
